<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/generate-commands.sh — edit the master in scripts/agent-refs/ instead. -->
# Polyrepo Git & Worktree Mechanics

Conductor supports two topologies. **Everything in this file is conditional on
the project being in polyrepo mode** — in monorepo mode every command behaves
exactly as it always has, and you can ignore this file.

## Topology check (run first, on every git-touching command)

1. Read `conductor/repos.json`.
   - **Absent, or `"mode"` ≠ `"polyrepo"`** → **monorepo mode.** The repo root is
     the single working tree. Use the flat `git_branch` / `worktree_path` fields
     in `metadata.json`. Stop reading this file.
   - **Present with `"mode": "polyrepo"`** → **polyrepo mode.** Continue below.
2. In polyrepo mode the current repo is the **control repo**. It holds
   `conductor/`, `.beads/`, `.gitmodules`, and the merge-train CI. Product code
   lives in **submodules** listed in `repos.json` (`submodule_path` mirrors a
   `.gitmodules` entry; `.gitmodules` is authoritative for path+URL, `repos.json`
   layers `default_branch` / `enabled` on top).
3. `git submodule status` should list every enabled repo. If a submodule is
   uninitialized, run `git submodule update --init <submodule_path>` (gated — ask
   the user) before operating on it.

## Resolving a task's repo

Every task in `plan.md` may carry a `<!-- repo: <name> -->` annotation
(parallel to `<!-- files: -->` / `<!-- depends: -->`). Resolution rule:

- Annotation present → that repo (must match a `repos[].name` in `repos.json`).
- Annotation absent → `repos.json.default_repo` (the backward-compat anchor).

Never strip the `<!-- repo: -->` annotation when marking a task complete — it is
how SHAs, branches, and worktrees are disambiguated for the life of the track.

## Per-repo metadata

`metadata.json` carries a `repos` map in polyrepo mode (the flat
`git_branch`/`worktree_path` fields are retained for monorepo):

```json
"repos": {
  "api": { "submodule_path": "repos/api", "git_branch": "track/<id>", "worktree_path": ".worktrees/<id>/api", "base_branch": "main" },
  "web": { "submodule_path": "repos/web", "git_branch": "track/<id>", "worktree_path": ".worktrees/<id>/web", "base_branch": "main" }
}
```

**Read rule:** `repos` present → per-repo lookup; else flat fields (monorepo).

## Worktree layout (polyrepo)

- Track worktree:   `.worktrees/<track_id>/<repo>/`
- Worker worktree:  `.worktrees/<track_id>/<repo>_worker_<N>_<name>/`

(Monorepo layout — `.worktrees/<track_id>` and
`.worktrees/<track_id>_worker_<N>_<name>` — is unchanged.)

Worktrees of a submodule must be created **with the submodule as the git
context**, because the submodule's real git dir is `.git/modules/<name>`, not a
nested `.git`.

Cursor's worktree automation assumes one repo. In polyrepo mode create
worktrees yourself with `git -C <submodule_path> worktree add … -b track/<id> <base_branch>`
and point the subagent at that path explicitly.

**Degraded fallback (documented):** if worktree-of-submodule proves unreliable
in your environment, operate directly on the submodule checkout with a per-repo
`track/<id>` branch (no worktree). Record the plain submodule path as
`worktree_path` so downstream commands still resolve it. Correctness first.

## Per-repo commit, branch, revert

- **Commit:** run inside the resolved repo's worktree (or `git -C <worktree>`).
  Record the 7-char SHA in `plan.md` as `[x] <sha7>`. The `<!-- repo: -->`
  annotation tells you which repo it belongs to. A rare task touching two repos
  records `[x] api:abc1234 web:def5678` (discouraged — prefer one task per repo).
- **Branch:** every touched repo uses the same name `track/<id>` on its own
  history. Branches are independent across repos.
- **Code is NEVER pushed by implement.** All product-repo commits stay local
  until `/conductor-land` (or archive's safety-net push) sends branch heads up
  for PRs. Only the control plane (`conductor/` + `.beads/`) is pushed earlier,
  and only in shared sync mode.
- **Revert (per-repo chains):** group recorded SHAs by repo. Within each repo run
  `git -C <submodule_path> revert --no-edit <sha>` in reverse chronological
  order. **Halt and report on the first conflict in any repo — never auto-continue
  across repos.** Reopen Beads tasks only after every repo's revert succeeds.

## Teardown

Loop over `metadata.json.repos` and remove each per-repo worktree
(`git -C <submodule_path> worktree remove <worktree_path>`), then prune. In
monorepo mode remove the single flat worktree as before.
